steps.title: Identify the goal
steps.content: The student wants to begin a narrative about one of the athletes. A narrative opening sets a scene with a moment in time — not a stat dump or a comparison.

steps.title: Match to the choice
steps.content:
The sentence about Martin putting on his Patriots uniform for his first NFL game opens with a vivid scene and introduces what would become a legendary career. That's classic narrative framing.

Comparisons of both athletes do not focus on one athlete, and a broad biographical summary does not begin with a narrative scene.

That is the correct answer.
